raw_consumer_statement:
On XX/XX/year>, I made XXXX separate XXXX purchases for XXXX tickets. Due to what appeared to be a platform glitch or error, I believed the first order had not processed and placed a second one. I attempted to cancel immediately upon realizing both had gone through, but XXXX does not allow cancellationseven within minutes of purchaseand does not publish or clearly state any refund policy window. 

I attempted to cancel through multiple channels : XXXX 's live chat, email ticket, and phone supportincluding on the day of the concert. Each time, I was told no cancellation was possible. XXXX later cited a missed refund window in communications with both me and Citibank, yet this window is not listed anywhere in their terms or visible at the point of sale. 

XXXX advised me to resell the tickets through their platform, which charges additional fees and did not result in a successful sale. I also attempted to sell externally, where I was defrauded by a buyer. I filed a police report, which Citi refused to accept as part of my dispute. 

I filed a dispute with Citi for {$5900.00} in XX/XX/year>. The claim was initially credited, then reversed. Although a Citi representative acknowledged it was misclassified, it was denied again under a second classification. At no point was I able to contact the Citi investigation team directly. This led to repeated delays, incorrect information, and ultimately an unfair denial. 

The dispute classification system obstructed fair resolution, and XXXX 's opaque policies exposed me to financial harm with no accountability. I received no service or refund, and both XXXX and Citi profited from fees while I was left responsible for a nearly {$6000.00} charge.
